Test Case Title: Join a pool and dynamically flip its MOQ status to "MOQ Met"
Preconditions: App loaded at baseline; Oxytocin pool at 7,500/10,000 units, status Filling
Test Steps:
1) Read initial progress text and status badge. 
2) Enter 3,000 in the pool's contribution input. 
3) Click Join Pool.
Expected Result: Progress text updates to 10,500 / 10,000 units filled; progress bar visually caps at width:100%; status badge flips to "MOQ Met"; success toast confirms the added units.

Test Case Title: Pool stays "Filling" or promotes to "Near MOQ" based on contribution size vs the 85% threshold
Test Steps:
1) Contribute 500 units to Amoxicillin. 
2) Contribute 2,500 units to Rotavirus.
Expected Result: Amoxicillin remains "Filling" (31% of MOQ); Rotavirus flips to "Near MOQ" (86% of MOQ, crosses the 85% threshold).

Test Case Title: Empty contribution is rejected with an error toast and pool total is unchanged
Test Steps: 1) Clear the contribution input for a pool. 2) Click Join Pool without typing a value.
Expected Result: Error toast reads "Enter a valid quantity..."; pool progress text/bar/status remain at their original baseline values.

Test Case Title: Gates Foundation MNCH Fund previews and applies a 1:1 match, doubling purchasing power
Preconditions: RFQ form empty; Gates Fund balance at $50,000
Test Steps:
1) Select Rotavirus Vaccine, quantity 1,000. 
2) Verify computed order value ($3,100). 
3) Select Gates Foundation MNCH Matching Fund. 
4) Verify match preview text and amounts. 
5) Submit.
Expected Result: Preview shows "Gates Foundation will match this purchase 1:1... $3,100 matched, $6,200 total purchasing power"; on submit, Gates balance decreases to $46,900; toast confirms Gates-financed order; Recent Orders gains an entry.

Test Case Title: RIFF revolving line draws down correctly for a financed order
Preconditions: RFQ form empty; RIFF balance at $250,000
Test Steps:
1) Select Amoxicillin 250mg, quantity 5,000. 
2) Select RIFF financing. 
3) Verify RIFF preview text. 
4) Submit.
Expected Result: RIFF preview shows "Drawing from RIFF: $250,000 currently available"; on submit, RIFF balance decreases to $249,600; toast confirms RIFF-financed order.

Test Case Title: RIFF-financed order is blocked with an error toast when it exceeds the available balance
Preconditions: RFQ form empty; RIFF balance at $250,000
Test Steps:
1) Select Rotavirus Vaccine, quantity 999,999 (order value ~$3.1M). 
2) Select RIFF financing. 
3) Submit.
Expected Result: Error toast reads "RIFF balance insufficient..."; RIFF balance remains unchanged at $250,000; no order added to Recent Orders.

Test Case Title: Cold-chain shipments display temperature readings matching the 2.0-6.5C SOP threshold
Preconditions: Logistics tracker loaded with 3 cold-chain shipments (SHP-2291, SHP-2288, SHP-2265)
Test Steps:
1) Read each cold-chain shipment's temperature badge. 
2) Derive expected STABLE/EXCURSION label from the 2.0-6.5C threshold. 
3) Compare to displayed label.
Expected Result: SHP-2291 (2.4C) and SHP-2265 (3.1C) show STABLE; SHP-2288 (6.8C) shows EXCURSION - matching the threshold-derived expectation.

Test Case Title: Non-cold-chain shipment shows "Ambient - N/A" instead of a temperature reading
Preconditions: Logistics tracker loaded; SHP-2276 (Amoxicillin) has coldChain=false
Test Steps: 1) Read SHP-2276's temperature badge.
Expected Result: Badge reads exactly "Ambient - N/A".

Test Case Title: Every tracked shipment shows the correct regulatory compliance badge
Test Steps: 1) Read the compliance badge for each of the 4 shipments.
Expected Result: 3 shipments show "FDA Ghana Approved"; SHP-2288 shows "Pending FDA Clearance".

Test Case Title: Advancing a shipment milestone to "Delivered" auto-reconciles a pending compliance status
Preconditions: SHP-2288 at milestone "Customs Clearing", compliance "Pending FDA Clearance"
Test Steps:
1) Click Advance on SHP-2288. 
2) Read milestone, toast, and compliance badge.
Expected Result: Milestone updates to "Delivered"; toast confirms the transition; compliance badge auto-updates to "FDA Ghana Approved".

Test Case Title: Pool contribution rejects empty, zero, negative, whitespace-only, and non-numeric input
Test Steps: For each of: empty string, "0", "-500", "   ", "abc" - type into a pool's contribution input and click Join Pool.
Expected Result: Every case shows the "Enter a valid quantity" error toast; the pool's progress text/bar/status remain exactly at the original baseline (no partial mutation).

Test Case Title: Decimal contribution is silently truncated (parseInt behavior), not rejected
Test Steps:
1) Enter "150.9" into the contribution input. 
2) Click Join Pool.
Expected Result: New total reads 4,350 / 15,000 (150 added, .9 truncated); success toast confirms the addition. Documented current behavior, not a defect fix request.

Test Case Title: Contribution far exceeding the MOQ does not break the progress bar or badge
Test Steps:
1) Enter "999999999" into the contribution input. 
2) Click Join Pool.
Expected Result: Progress bar visually clamps at width:100% (never overflows); status badge reads "MOQ Met"; progress text renders the full formatted number without NaN/undefined.

Test Case Title: RFQ form's native HTML5 validation blocks every invalid submit combination before any app JS runs
Test Steps: For each of: (no pool + no quantity), (pool selected + quantity="0"), (pool selected + quantity="-10") - fill accordingly and click Submit Purchase Order.
Expected Result: form.checkValidity() is false in every case; the success toast never appears (stays hidden); no entry is added to Recent Orders - the browser blocks submission via required/min=1 constraints before the app's JS submit handler executes.

Test Case Title: RFQ form accepts the exact boundary value at the minimum (quantity = 1)
Test Steps:
1) Select a pool. 
2) Enter quantity "1". 
3) Submit.
Expected Result: form.checkValidity() is true; order submits successfully as self-funded; Recent Orders gains exactly 1 entry.

Test Case Title: Demand pool contributions do NOT persist across a page reload (no storage layer)
Test Steps:
1) Contribute to a pool and confirm the total updates. 
2) Reload the page. 
3) Re-read the pool's progress text.
Expected Result: After reload, the pool total reverts to the original fixture baseline - documents the current lack of a persistence layer as a regression tripwire, not a bug report.

Test Case Title: Mock user session is static markup, identical before and after reload
Test Steps:
1) Read logged-in user name/role. 
2) Reload. 
3) Re-read name/role.
Expected Result: "Amara Okoye" / "Regional Procurement Hub Manager" shown both times - unchanged because it's hardcoded, not restored by any session mechanism.

Test Case Title: Multiple RFQ submissions accumulate correctly in Recent Orders, most recent first
Test Steps:
1) Submit a self-funded order for Oxytocin. 
2) Submit a second self-funded order for Amoxicillin.
Expected Result: Recent Orders shows 2 entries; the Amoxicillin entry appears first (most recent), Oxytocin second - no overwriting or duplication.

Test Case Title: Toast singleton shows the latest message, not a stale one, across rapid sequential actions
Test Steps:
1) Trigger an error toast (empty pool contribution). 
2) Immediately trigger a success action (valid contribution) on the same pool.
Expected Result: Toast message updates to reflect the success action; it does not remain stuck on the earlier error text.

Test Case Title: Pool aggregation, financing, and logistics state remain isolated from one another
Test Steps:
1) Read RIFF balance and SHP-2291 milestone. 
2) Contribute to a pool; re-check RIFF balance and milestone. 
3) Advance SHP-2291's milestone; re-check pool total and RIFF balance.
Expected Result: A pool contribution never changes financing or logistics data; a milestone advance never changes pool totals or financing balances - the three subsystems are fully isolated in state.

Test Case Title: App remains functionally usable when the Tailwind CDN fails to load entirely
Preconditions: cy.intercept forces a network error on the Tailwind CDN script
Test Steps:
1) Visit the app with the CDN blocked. 
2) Contribute to a pool as normal.
Expected Result: Despite an uncaught ReferenceError from the failed CDN script, the later business-logic script still runs; the pool join succeeds and updates the UI normally (unstyled but functional).

Test Case Title: App still becomes interactive after a slow (3s-delayed) CDN response
Preconditions: cy.intercept delays the Tailwind CDN response by 3000ms
Test Steps:
1) Visit the app. 
2) Wait for and check that key elements become visible/interactive within a bounded timeout.
Expected Result: Page finishes loading and the pool join button / user name are visible and correct within ~10s - no indefinite hang.

Test Case Title: Header widgets hide/reveal at the exact Tailwind sm (640px) / md (768px) breakpoints
Preconditions: App loaded at 6 defined viewport widths: 375, 639, 640, 767, 768, 1280
Test Steps: For each viewport: set the viewport size, visit the app, check visibility of the system-date widget and the user name/role block.
Expected Result: System-date hidden below 640px, visible at/above; user info hidden below 768px, visible at/above - matching the hidden/sm:flex and hidden/md:block Tailwind classes exactly at the pixel boundary.

Test Case Title: All three core panels remain reachable on a small mobile viewport (375x667)
Test Steps:
1) Visit the app. 
2) Scroll to and check visibility of the Demand Pool, Financing, and Logistics panels.
Expected Result: All three panels are present and become visible when scrolled into view - layout reflows but nothing is lost off-canvas.

Test Case Title: Pool-join interaction fully functions on a mobile viewport (375x667), not just visually
Test Steps:
1) Visit the app. 
2) Contribute to a pool exactly as on desktop.
Expected Result: Contribution succeeds and the success toast appears, confirming functional (not just visual) parity on mobile.

Test Case Title: Pool-join interaction functions identically on a large desktop viewport (1920x1080)
Test Steps:
1) Visit the app. 
2) Contribute to a pool.
Expected Result: Contribution succeeds and the success toast appears, confirming parity with the mobile result.

Test Case Title: Full suite renders and passes consistently across Chrome, Firefox, and Edge
Preconditions: cy:run:chrome / cy:run:firefox / cy:run:edge npm scripts wired into CI
Test Steps: Run the entire spec directory once per browser engine via the CI matrix (not a single in-spec assertion).
Expected Result: Same pass/fail outcome across all three engines; any browser-specific rendering/JS difference surfaces as a failure in that browser's run.

Test Case Title: Every currency amount renders with a hardcoded "$" symbol (baseline)
Test Steps:
1) Read RIFF and Gates balances. 
2) Match against ^\$[0-9,]+$ pattern.
Expected Result: Both balances match the pattern exactly - confirms today's hardcoded USD-only formatting as a regression baseline.

Test Case Title: Header date renders as static hardcoded text, not a computed/localised date
Test Steps: 1) Read the system-date widget's text.
Expected Result: Text contains the literal hardcoded string "Tuesday, 7 July 2026" regardless of the actual current date.

Test Case Title: Compliance and status badges render in English only, with no translation layer
Test Steps:
1) Check compliance badges contain "FDA Ghana Approved" / "Pending FDA Clearance". 
2) Check a pool status badge is one of the known English labels.
Expected Result: All badges show hardcoded English strings - confirms there is no i18n layer to accidentally regress.

Test Case Title: Number grouping follows the browser's bare default locale via unlocalized toLocaleString() (latent risk)
Preconditions: App loaded at baseline, test runner locale = en-US
Test Steps: 1) Read a pool's progress text and check it includes a comma thousands separator.
Expected Result: Comma grouping is present today under en-US; flagged as a latent risk because every toLocaleString() call omits an explicit locale argument, so output would silently shift under a different OS/browser locale.
